title: Add touch/pointer event support to Blazor views and revamp sample app
body:
## Description

Adds touch/pointer event support to the Blazor `SKCanvasView` and `SKGLView` components, and revamps the Blazor WebAssembly sample app into a three-page showcase of SkiaSharp capabilities.

- Fixes #3534

## Touch/pointer event system

### Shared touch types

`SKTouchAction`, `SKTouchDeviceType`, `SKMouseButton`, and `SKTouchEventArgs` are moved from `SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Core` to `SkiaSharp.Views.Shared` with `#if __BLAZOR__` / `#if __MAUI__` namespace switching — the same shared-file pattern used by `SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs`.

### JS interop (`SKTouchInterop.ts`)

A class-based module (matching the `SizeWatcher`/`DpiWatcher` pattern) that:

- Listens for `pointer*` and `wheel` events on the canvas element.
- Maps browser events to `SKTouchAction`/`SKMouseButton`/`SKTouchDeviceType` enum values.
- Invokes the C# callback **synchronously** and reads back the `Handled` return value.
- When `Handled` is `true`: calls `preventDefault()` + `stopPropagation()` (e.g., blocks page scrolling for handled wheel events).
- When `Handled` is `false`: browser default behavior is preserved.
- `start()` adds all listeners with `setPointerCapture`; `stop()` removes them and releases capture.
- Normalizes wheel delta to the **v120 standard** (120 = one mouse notch) across browsers and platforms.

### C# interop (`SKTouchInterop.cs`)

Inherits from `JSModuleInterop` with `#if NET7_0_OR_GREATER` split:

- **NET7+**: `[JSImport]` static partial methods with `Func<JSObject, bool>` callback. The `JSObject` proxy is disposed via `using var _` after property extraction to prevent GC/finalizer pressure from high-frequency pointer events.
- **Pre-NET7**: `DotNetObjectReference<SKTouchCallbackHelper>` with `[JSInvokable] bool OnPointerEvent(...)`.

### View changes (`SKCanvasView` & `SKGLView`)

New component parameters:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `Touch` | `Action<SKTouchEventArgs>?` | Callback for pointer/touch/wheel events |
| `EnableTouchEvents` | `bool` | Opt-in; when `false` (default), no JS listeners are attached |

The JS module is imported on first render but listeners are **not** attached until `EnableTouchEvents` is set to `true`. Toggling at runtime calls `Start()`/`Stop()` to fully connect/disconnect all JS event listeners.

## Blazor sample app revamp

The basic Blazor WebAssembly sample is rebuilt from the ground up into three pages:

| Page | What it demonstrates |
|------|---------------------|
| **Home** | CPU-rendered gradient background with animated circles, text rendering with `SKFont`/`SKTextBlob`, and `SKCanvasView` basics |
| **GPU** | `SKGLView` with a real-time SkSL metaball shader, touch interaction (drag to attract blobs), and FPS overlay |
| **Drawing** | Multi-touch drawing canvas with stroke recording, color picker toolbar, wheel-to-resize brush, and undo support |



### Sample features exercised

- `SKCanvasView` with `OnPaintSurface` (Home)
- `SKGLView` with `EnableRenderLoop` and `OnPaintSurface` (GPU)
- Touch events: `Pressed`, `Moved`, `Released`, `WheelChanged` (Drawing, GPU)
- `Handled` feedback loop — blocks page scroll during drawing, allows scroll on Home
- `SKRuntimeEffect.BuildShader` with SkSL (GPU)
- `SKPath` stroke recording and replay (Drawing)
- Scoped `.razor.css` for component-level styling (GPU, Drawing)


## API Changes

**Added:**

- `enum SKTouchAction { Entered, Pressed, Moved, Released, Cancelled, Exited, WheelChanged }` (shared)
- `enum SKTouchDeviceType { Touch, Mouse, Pen }` (shared)
- `enum SKMouseButton { Unknown, Left, Middle, Right }` (shared)
- `class SKTouchEventArgs : EventArgs` — Id, ActionType, DeviceType, MouseButton, Location, InContact, WheelDelta, Pressure, Handled (shared)
- `Action<SKTouchEventArgs>? SKCanvasView.Touch`
- `bool SKCanvasView.EnableTouchEvents`
- `Action<SKTouchEventArgs>? SKGLView.Touch`
- `bool SKGLView.EnableTouchEvents`

**Moved (no breaking change):**

- `SKTouchEventArgs`, `SKTouchAction`, `SKTouchDeviceType`, `SKMouseButton` moved from `SkiaSharp.Views.Maui.Core/SKTouchEventArgs.cs` to `SkiaSharp.Views.Shared/SKTouchEventArgs.cs` with conditional namespace via `#if __BLAZOR__` / `#if __MAUI__`

## Behavioral Changes

Touch events are opt-in via `EnableTouchEvents="true"`. When `false` (default), no JS event listeners are attached. **Existing usage is unaffected.**

When touch events are enabled:
- `e.Handled = true` → JS calls `preventDefault()`/`stopPropagation()` on the browser event
- `e.Handled = false` (default) → browser default behavior is preserved

## Usage

```razor
<SKCanvasView OnPaintSurface="OnPaintSurface"
              Touch="OnTouch" EnableTouchEvents="true" />

@code {
    void OnTouch(SKTouchEventArgs e)
    {
        var point = e.Location;    // CSS pixels
        var device = e.DeviceType; // Touch, Mouse, or Pen
        e.Handled = true;          // preventDefault + stopPropagation in browser
    }
}
```

## PR Checklist

- [ ] Has tests (no existing Blazor test project; recommend `tests/SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.Tests` with bUnit)
- [x] Rebased on top of main
- [x] Changes adhere to coding standard
- [x] Updated sample app

comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 we need to add this to the GL view as well.

Added the same `Touch` EventCallback, `EnableTouchEvents` parameter, touch interop initialization, and `IAsyncDisposable` to `SKGLView` in commit 20724c4.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot Please look at how we did interop for other features and make sure to match that.

Refactored all touch interop to match the existing patterns in commit d047799:

- `SKTouchInterop` inherits from `JSModuleInterop` with `#if NET7_0_OR_GREATER` split (like `SizeWatcherInterop`, `DpiWatcherInterop`)
- `SKTouchCallbackHelper` guarded with `#if !NET7_0_OR_GREATER` (like `ActionHelper`, `FloatFloatActionHelper`)
- `Touch` parameter changed to `Action<SKTouchEventArgs>?` (like `OnPaintSurface`)
- TypeScript rewritten as class-based module with `invokeMethod` (sync) and `typeof callback === 'function'` pattern (like `SizeWatcher`, `DpiWatcher`)
- No more `IAsyncDisposable` — disposal goes through `JSModuleInterop.Dispose()`

> [!WARNING]
>

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot Please update the blazor basic sample to now use these touch events and remove the other attributes.
> 
> Also address the comments

Updated the Raster sample to use `Touch="OnTouch" EnableTouchEvents="true"` instead of `@onpointerdown`/`@onpointermove`/`@onpointerup` attributes. Also addressed the EnableTouchEvents toggle comment — touch interop is now always registered, with a JS-side `enabled` flag toggled via `setEnabled()`. Commit 5b48423.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot we need to consider the defults and try totally disconnect. But also, all of this should be tested using bUnit in a new test project. Please investigate what we have and what we should do. Th...

Addressed the disconnect approach — `EnableTouchEvents` now toggles `Start()`/`Stop()` to fully attach/detach all JS event listeners, and the default `false` skips registration entirely. Commit 4c23902.

Regarding bUnit: There's currently no Blazor test project in the repo. bUnit (https://bunit.dev) supports testing Blazor components in isolation without a browser. It can mock `IJSRuntime` and verify component rendering, parameter changes, and event callbacks. For `SKCanvasView`/`SKGLView`, bUnit could test:
- Component renders a `<canvas>` element
- `Touch` callback fires when `OnPointerEvent` is invoked
- `EnableTouchEvents` toggle calls `Start()`/`Stop()` on the interop
- Parameters like `IgnorePixelScaling`, `EnableRenderLoop` propagate correctly

A new `tests/SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor.Tests` project referencing `bunit` + `xunit` would be the right setup. This would need the `wasm-tools` workload and JSInterop mocking. Happy to create the test project structure if you'd like.

comments.body: Closing this as we do not want to be adding APIs to platforms. And there is no need to actually, the events are fine.
